title: Marriage Story
logline: A theater director and his actress wife navigate a coast-to-coast divorce while trying to maintain their relationship as co-parents to their young son.
antagonistOrConflict: The divorce process and legal system that transforms their amicable separation into an adversarial battle
tone: Intimate, bittersweet, emotionally raw with moments of dark comedy
themes: The destructive nature of legal proceedings

actBreaks.description: Nicole reveals she's staying in LA and has hired a lawyer, transforming their amicable separation into a legal battle.

validation.ruling: Charlie setting up the couch as his bed while surveying their shared objects represents the irreversible end of their marriage's ordinary state, fundamentally changing the story direction as they transition from troubled couple to separated parents.

actBreaks.description: The court hearing concludes with the judge's ruling, marking the legal resolution of their custody dispute.

validation.ruling: The courtroom scene where Jay argues Charlie deserves half of Nicole's TV earnings while Nora objects to slut-shaming creates the final escalation point, forcing both characters into the highest-stakes personal conflict that cannot be undone.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a deceptively simple three-act structure that mirrors the emotional journey of divorce. Act I establishes the couple's loving dynamic through contrasting voiceovers, then shatters it when Nicole reveals her legal maneuvering. Act II becomes a brutal war of attrition as both characters are consumed by the legal process, culminating in Charlie's complete breakdown. Act III focuses on rebuilding and acceptance, showing how they learn to co-parent despite their wounds. The structure is elegant in its restraint—no artificial plot devices, just the inexorable logic of two people trying to untangle their lives.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately methodical in the first act, building intimacy through mundane moments before the devastating revelation at scene 32. The middle act accelerates through legal machinations and emotional confrontations, peaking with the explosive argument at scene 74. The final act slows to a contemplative pace, allowing for healing and resolution through smaller, more intimate scenes.

incitingIncident.description: A mediator reveals that the loving descriptions we just heard were written exercises, and Nicole refuses to read hers aloud, saying she doesn't like what she wrote.

validation.ruling: The mediator's office scene shatters the ordinary world by revealing Charlie and Nicole are divorcing, forcing them into irreversible conflict when Nicole refuses to participate and storms out, establishing the main story goal of navigating their separation.

midpoint.description: Charlie desperately calls someone on the phone while walking to his car with Henry, needing a lawyer immediately or he could lose custody by tomorrow.

validation.ruling: Scene 52 is the true midpoint where Charlie desperately calls for a lawyer while walking with Henry, realizing he could lose custody by tomorrow - this forces him from reactive to proactive, creating a point of no return where he must fight aggressively rather than hope for amicable resolution.

climax.description: Charlie prepares an elaborate dinner for the custody evaluator Nancy Katz, who visits his decorated apartment and asks detailed questions about his daily routine and parenting while Henry plays nearby.

validation.ruling: Scene 77 represents the climax where Charlie faces the custody evaluator Nancy Katz in his carefully prepared apartment, demonstrating his character growth and applying lessons learned - this is his final, defining confrontation to prove his worthiness as a father and resolve the main custody question.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. MANHATTAN. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole appears in shadow as Charlie's voiceover describes her ability to make people comfortable and how she really listens.

scenes.heading: EXT. FLATBUSH AVENUE, PARK SLOPE, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole exits a subway and stops to listen to an ASPCA solicitor's pitch, writing down her phone number as Charlie's voiceover continues praising her character.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole encourages a sulking Charlie to make a phone call, eventually convincing him to take the phone.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole cuts Henry's hair, then Charlie's, then her own while the family plays and cleans up together.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. SEVERAL DAYS
scenes.summary: A kettle whistles as we see multiple untouched cups of tea throughout the apartment and Charlie cleaning up after Nicole's habit of leaving cabinets open.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie opens a present from Nicole - a trumpet - as his voiceover praises her gift-giving and playful parenting.

scenes.heading: EXT. PROSPECT PARK, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole and Henry play with Star Wars figures in the park, making sound effects for their battles.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole, Charlie and Henry play Monopoly, with Nicole getting frustrated about going to jail and warning them not to laugh at her.

scenes.heading: INT. HENRY’S ROOM. ANOTHER NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nicole reads to Henry in bed, falling asleep while still managing to continue reading.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nicole puts on music and gets people dancing at a theater cast party.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S KITCHEN. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie struggles unsuccessfully to open a jar of pickles, trying various methods.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie watches Nicole on TV in a movie where she's about to lift her shirt at a college party, as his voiceover explains she gave up potential movie stardom to do theater with him.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole rehearses on stage, climbing up an actor's body like a rock-face while Charlie directs from offstage.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. SAME DAY
scenes.summary: The perspective shifts to Charlie in close-up as Nicole's voiceover begins, describing how he's undaunted by others' opinions.

scenes.heading: INT. PIZZERIA, PARK SLOPE, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie eats messily with Henry at a pizza place, then helps Henry fix his backwards sweater.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie accidentally turns off the lights while Nicole is reading, then turns them back on and apologizes.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOVIE THEATER. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie and Henry exit a movie theater both crying, comparing how many times they cried during the film.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S KITCHEN. EVE
scenes.summary: Charlie cooks an elaborate dinner with multiple pots while Henry helps by cutting peppers.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole rages about something while Charlie remains steady and doesn't react to her mood.

scenes.heading: INT. TENNIS BUBBLE. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie double faults while serving in a tennis game and angrily tells himself to get it together.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S BEDROOM. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Henry wakes Charlie from sleep to tell him about a bad dream.

scenes.heading: INT. HENRY’S ROOM. SAME
scenes.summary: Charlie puts Henry back to sleep through a series of compromises, ending up sleeping in Henry's bed together.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBWAY/PLATFORM. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie and Henry read on the subway until Charlie suddenly realizes they've missed their stop.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nicole picks food from her teeth at a restaurant table while Charlie watches from off-camera.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie leads a meeting with his theater team around a stage model while Henry plays on the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie distributes personalized coffee orders to all theater company members and remembers inside jokes with an intern.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. PREVIOUS DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie watches Nicole rehearse from the back of the theater, taking notes under a reading light, and nods approvingly when she looks to him for feedback.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDIATOR’S OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: A mediator reveals that the loving descriptions we just heard were written exercises, and Nicole refuses to read hers aloud, saying she doesn't like what she wrote.
scenes.turningPointNote: The reveal that the opening loving portraits were divorce mediation exercises completely recontextualizes everything we've seen and establishes the central conflict.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATER. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nicole performs on stage in a red dress with video projections, delivering lines lifelessly while Charlie watches sadly from the back of the theater.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT. NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the closing night party, the theater company celebrates while Charlie and Nicole sit at separate tables, with Mary Ann asking Donna when she can approach Charlie's table.

scenes.heading: INT. Q TRAIN SUBWAY
scenes.summary: Nicole and Charlie ride the subway home separately - she sits gazing out the window while he stands by the doors despite available seats.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Charlie and Nicole return home early to relieve their babysitter, and Nicole discovers Henry's packed suitcase on the floor while checking on their sleeping son.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of Henry's packed suitcase reveals Nicole's plan to leave, escalating the separation from emotional distance to concrete action.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S CHILDHOOD BEDROOM, LOS ANGELES. MORNING
scenes.summary: Nicole wakes up in her childhood bedroom in LA with Henry beside her as her mother Sandra loudly opens curtains and announces plans for Nicole's hair/makeup test and Henry's Fairy Camp.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. TV STUDIO. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole undergoes a hair and makeup test for a TV show while producers discuss her decade in New York theater, and she tries unsuccessfully to interject into their conversation.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SOUNDSTAGE. LATER
scenes.summary: Nicole walks across the soundstage with the producers who discuss the show's early 2000s aesthetic and mention her old movie being on TV.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S TRAILER
scenes.summary: The female producer follows Nicole into her trailer and gives her a divorce lawyer's phone number, revealing she divorced the male producer but still works with him.

scenes.heading: INT. NORA FANSHAW’S OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole meets with divorce lawyer Nora Fanshaw in her elegant office, where Nora praises Nicole's acting and discusses seeing Charlie's theater work.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nicole's decision to consult a divorce lawyer marks the transition from separation to active legal proceedings.

scenes.heading: INT. SANDRA’S HOUSE, LOS ANGELES. EVENING
scenes.summary: Henry plays Monopoly with his cousins and uncle while having trouble reading a Chance card.

scenes.heading: INT. SANDRA’S KITCHEN. SAME
scenes.summary: Nicole, Cassie, and Sandra prepare to serve Charlie divorce papers, drinking wine while discussing who will hand him the envelope and where Henry should be during the exchange.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S ROOM AT SANDRA’S
scenes.summary: Charlie, Nicole, and Henry lie in bed together as Charlie finishes reading Stuart Little, with both parents crying at the ending before Charlie leaves the room at Henry's request.

scenes.heading: INT. JAY MAROTTA’S LAW OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie meets with aggressive lawyer Jay Marotta who demands a $25,000 retainer and explains his high fees while an associate takes notes.

scenes.heading: INT. BROADWAY REHEARSAL SPACE, NEW YORK. DAY
scenes.summary: Actors rehearse Charlie's Broadway production while discussing Nicole's absence, with some mentioning her pilot went to series and Henry starting school in LA.

scenes.heading: INT. REHEARSAL SPACE HALLWAY. SAME
scenes.summary: Mary Ann gives Charlie rehearsal notes and blueprints, then asks to come over, but Charlie declines saying he can't be with anyone right now despite their previous affair.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL. SAME
scenes.summary: Nora calls Charlie in a theater stairwell to inform him he's missed the 30-day deadline to respond to the divorce filing, while he tries to manage rehearsal decisions simultaneously.

scenes.heading: EXT. RENTAL CAR AREA, LAX AIRPORT. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie wanders aimlessly through a rental car parking lot at LAX airport.

scenes.heading: INT. RENTAL CAR. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie drives the rental car, struggling with the air conditioning and being blinded by the sun through the windshield.

scenes.heading: EXT. SANDRA’S HOUSE, WEST HOLLYWOOD, LOS ANGELES, DAY
scenes.summary: Henry runs out to greet Charlie but doesn't hug him, focused on a treasure hunt with Nicole, who now has blonde hair, leading to Charlie asking to speak with Nicole privately about her lawyer's threats.

scenes.heading: INT. RENTAL CAR. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie drives with a bleeding hand while Henry asks him to spell 'Lego Bionicles' and announces he wants to be a store-bought ninja for Halloween instead of the Frankenstein costume Charlie had made.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. PARKING ENTRANCE
scenes.summary: Charlie struggles to reach the parking ticket button from his rental car, having to unbuckle and step out to push it.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE BUILDING LOBBY
scenes.summary: Charlie and Henry enter an office building lobby carrying travel bags, with Charlie explaining his discomfort about leaving bags with unknown valet staff.

scenes.heading: INT. LAW OFFICE
scenes.summary: Charlie arrives at a law office for an appointment with Dan Cohen, but the receptionist informs him that Cohen cannot represent him because Nicole already consulted with him on August 7th, creating a legal conflict of interest.

scenes.heading: EXT. MINI-MALL/FAST FOOD RESTAURANT
scenes.summary: Charlie desperately calls someone on the phone while walking to his car with Henry, needing a lawyer immediately or he could lose custody by tomorrow.

scenes.heading: INT. SANDRA’S HOUSE, BATHROOM, LOS ANGELES. INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Sandra secretly helps Charlie by whispering lawyer recommendations over the phone while running shower water to hide their conversation, giving him Bert Spitz's name.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND HOLLYWOOD BOULEVARD. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie and Henry arrive at a cramped parking lot behind a run-down building on Hollywood Boulevard to meet the lawyer.

scenes.heading: INT. BERT SPITZ’S LAW OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie meets with Bert Spitz, a four-time married divorce lawyer who promises a collaborative approach and claims to treat clients as people rather than transactions, while his grizzled cat wheezes nearby.
scenes.turningPointNote: Charlie secures legal representation, marking his entry into the formal divorce process

scenes.heading: INT. SANDRA’S HOUSE, LOS ANGELES
scenes.summary: Charlie brings Henry a Frankenstein costume to Sandra's house, but Nicole explains Henry wants to be a ninja like his cousins, creating tension over their conflicting Halloween plans.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Charlie sleeps in his Invisible Man costume in his hotel room until Nicole arrives to drop off Henry for Halloween, both parents in elaborate costumes.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE’S RENTAL CAR
scenes.summary: Charlie drives with Henry looking for trick-or-treating spots while Henry expresses preference for Los Angeles because he can sit in cars and likes his friends there better.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNSET BLVD
scenes.summary: The Invisible Man and ninja cross busy Sunset Boulevard and trudge up steep streets, but find no one answering doors for trick-or-treating.

scenes.heading: INT. PINK DOT
scenes.summary: Charlie and Henry resort to getting candy from a convenience store clerk after their unsuccessful trick-or-treating attempts.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE’S RENTAL CAR
scenes.summary: Charlie drives back to the hotel with Henry falling asleep in the backseat after their disappointing Halloween.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Back at the hotel, Henry dumps out his meager Halloween haul while Charlie tells him he has to return to New York on Monday, and Henry asks why Charlie isn't there more often.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL BALCONY. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Charlie steps onto his hotel balcony to make a phone call asking someone if they're moving out to Los Angeles.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLLYWOOD HALLOWEEN PARTY. INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Charlie and Nicole have a heated phone argument between his hotel balcony and her Hollywood party, where she refuses to discuss their situation directly and tells him to have his lawyer call Nora.

scenes.heading: INT. PABLO’S GRIP TRUCK
scenes.summary: Nicole makes out with Pablo in his grip truck, specifically requesting only fingering while declaring she's changing her whole life.

scenes.heading: INT. CONFERENCE ROOM, CENTURY CITY, LA. DAY
scenes.summary: In a conference room, Charlie and Nicole sit at separate tables listening to their lawyers argue about custody and jurisdiction, with Nora emphasizing Nicole's LA roots and Bert defending Charlie's New York position.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALLER CONFERENCE ROOM
scenes.summary: Bert explains to Charlie in a smaller conference room that he's in a legal bind because flying to LA to see Henry sets a precedent, but not coming would make him look uncaring to a judge.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE’S RENTAL APARTMENT. EVENING
scenes.summary: Charlie sits in his bare rental apartment paying legal bills including $25,000 to Bert's firm while Henry video chats with Nicole about power outages and a broken gate.

scenes.heading: EXT. NICOLE’S NEW HOUSE, ECHO PARK. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Charlie brings Henry to Nicole's new house where the security gate is stuck open, and Nicole blocks Charlie from entering while directing him to an outside electrical panel.

scenes.heading: EXT. NICOLE’S PORCH
scenes.summary: Nicole cuts Charlie's hair on her porch under the porch light while he sits shirtless with a towel around his shoulders.

scenes.heading: EXT. NICOLE’S NEW PLACE. ECHO PARK, LA. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Nicole suggests the sleeping Henry stay at her place, but Charlie insists it's his night and takes Henry, with both parents helping to close the stuck gate that separates them.

scenes.heading: INT. LOS ANGELES COURTHOUSE, HALLWAY. MORNING
scenes.summary: In the courthouse hallway filled with arguing divorcing couples, Nora tells Nicole they've resolved ninety percent of the details and the judge should make the divorce official.

scenes.heading: INT. COURT ROOM. DAY
scenes.summary: In court, Jay Marotta argues that Charlie deserves half of Nicole's TV earnings since he helped build her career, while Nora objects to slut-shaming Nicole for her past film role.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE'S RENTAL APARTMENT. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie and Nicole meet at his rental apartment where their attempt at civil conversation about schedule changes escalates into a devastating screaming fight, with Nicole unleashing years of resentment and Charlie responding with cruel personal attacks.
scenes.turningPointNote: The explosive fight destroys any remaining civility between them and marks the complete breakdown of their relationship

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE’S RENTAL. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie receives rental furniture and plants for his apartment, hangs Henry's artwork, and buys board games while Henry questions the sudden abundance of plants.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole sits with an unseen custody evaluator, answering questions about jail time from a peace demonstration and her alcohol consumption habits.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE'S RENTAL APARTMENT. EVENING
scenes.summary: Charlie prepares an elaborate dinner for the custody evaluator Nancy Katz, who visits his decorated apartment and asks detailed questions about his daily routine and parenting while Henry plays nearby.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S NEW PLACE. ECHO PARK, LA. DAY
scenes.summary: At Nicole's housewarming party, she performs a musical number from Company with Cassie and Sandra for guests including family and coworkers in her newly decorated, colorful home.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S KITCHEN. LATER
scenes.summary: In Nicole's kitchen during the party, Nora opens champagne and celebrates that Charlie dropped his New York claim, meaning they're mostly done with the divorce proceedings.

scenes.heading: INT. NICOLE’S KITCHEN, LA. DAY
scenes.summary: Nicole signs divorce papers at her kitchen table in LA, using her maiden name Ryder.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARLIE AND NICOLE’S APARTMENT, NEW YORK. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie signs divorce papers while sitting on the floor of his half-empty New York apartment.
scenes.turningPointNote: The divorce is finalized with both parties signing the papers

scenes.heading: EXT. FLATBUSH AVENUE, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie bluntly tells an ASPCA fundraiser on the street that he doesn't care about animals.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNDROMAT, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie watches a family with a baby at a laundromat in Brooklyn.

scenes.heading: INT. BARBER SHOP, BROOKLYN. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie gets a haircut at a barber shop in Brooklyn.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Charlie joins his theater friends at a restaurant, buying drinks for everyone and complaining about how Nicole claimed ownership of their shared couch during the divorce.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. SANDRA’S HOUSE, WEST HOLLYWOOD, LOS ANGELES. DAY
scenes.summary: Charlie arrives at Sandra's house to pick up Henry, awkwardly meeting Nicole's boyfriend Carter during a laser tag game, then sits with the family for an uncomfortable dinner.

scenes.heading: EXT. PASADENA RESIDENTIAL STREETS, LA, LATE DAY
scenes.summary: During Halloween trick-or-treating, Nicole offers to let Charlie take the exhausted Henry even though it's her night, and Charlie accepts, with Nicole asking him to text when he's awake in the morning.
scenes.turningPointNote: Nicole's gesture of giving Charlie extra time with Henry signals a move toward post-divorce cooperation

structuralWarnings.text: Act 1 is only 11% of the script — structural placement suspect
2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
